item.name: Punchdrunk Punters Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
These beat up and well worn weapons smell as if they've spent a week at the bottom of a forgotten cask in a busy tavern. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Each time you deal damage to a creature, it gains one Toxin Point for 1 hour, to a maximum of 10 Toxin Points. As a Magic action, you can cause a creature you can see to take 1d8 Poison damage for each Toxin Point it has, then it loses all Toxin Points.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Ben Huffman - Pugilist, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Punchdrunk Punters Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
These beat up and well worn weapons smell as if they've spent a week at the bottom of a forgotten cask in a busy tavern. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Each time you deal damage to a creature, it gains one Toxin Point for 1 hour, to a maximum of 10 Toxin Points. As a Magic action, you can cause a creature you can see to take 1d8 Poison damage for each Toxin Point it has, then it loses all Toxin Points.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Ben Huffman - Pugilist, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Punchdrunk Punters Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
These beat up and well worn weapons smell as if they've spent a week at the bottom of a forgotten cask in a busy tavern. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Each time you deal damage to a creature, it gains one Toxin Point for 1 hour, to a maximum of 10 Toxin Points. As a Magic action, you can cause a creature you can see to take 1d8 Poison damage for each Toxin Point it has, then it loses all Toxin Points.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Ben Huffman - Pugilist, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Hellfire Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This weapon is fashioned from infernal iron and traced with veins of hellfire that shed dim light in a 5-foot-radius. Any humanoid killed by an attack made with this weapon has its soul funneled into the River Styx, where it's reborn instantly as a lemure devil.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Baldur's Gate: Descent Into Avernus p. 223, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Hellfire Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
This weapon is fashioned from infernal iron and traced with veins of hellfire that shed dim light in a 5-foot-radius. Any humanoid killed by an attack made with this weapon has its soul funneled into the River Styx, where it's reborn instantly as a lemure devil.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Baldur's Gate: Descent Into Avernus p. 223, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Hellfire Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
This weapon is fashioned from infernal iron and traced with veins of hellfire that shed dim light in a 5-foot-radius. Any humanoid killed by an attack made with this weapon has its soul funneled into the River Styx, where it's reborn instantly as a lemure devil.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Baldur's Gate: Descent Into Avernus p. 223, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Adamantine Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Adamantine Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Adamantine Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Brass Knuckles of Throne's Command [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This weapon is bedecked in ornate gold filigree and deep-blue and maroon jewels. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Additionally, you gain proficiency in the Intimidation and Persuasion skills if you don't already have it.
Spellcasting: The weapon has 5 charges. You can use a bonus action and expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 16): Command (1 charge), Zone of Truth (2 charges), Compulsion (4 charges), Banishment (4 charges), or Dominate Person (5 charges). The weapon regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: The Book of Many Things p. 39, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Hand Claws of Throne's Command [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
This weapon is bedecked in ornate gold filigree and deep-blue and maroon jewels. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Additionally, you gain proficiency in the Intimidation and Persuasion skills if you don't already have it.
Spellcasting: The weapon has 5 charges. You can use a bonus action and expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 16): Command (1 charge), Zone of Truth (2 charges), Compulsion (4 charges), Banishment (4 charges), or Dominate Person (5 charges). The weapon regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: The Book of Many Things p. 39, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Punch Knife of Throne's Command [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
This weapon is bedecked in ornate gold filigree and deep-blue and maroon jewels. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Additionally, you gain proficiency in the Intimidation and Persuasion skills if you don't already have it.
Spellcasting: The weapon has 5 charges. You can use a bonus action and expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 16): Command (1 charge), Zone of Truth (2 charges), Compulsion (4 charges), Banishment (4 charges), or Dominate Person (5 charges). The weapon regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: The Book of Many Things p. 39, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Dragon's Wrath Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. When it steeps in a dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the dragon's breath weapon and deals damage of that type with its special properties.
Slumbering (Uncommon): Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.
Stirring (Rare): The Stirring weapon has the Slumbering property. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra 1d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.
Wakened (Very Rare): The Wakened weapon has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring property. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +2, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6. As an action, you can unleash a 30-foot cone of destructive energy from the weapon. Each creature in that area must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Ascendant (Legendary): The Ascendant weapon has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring and Wakened properties. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +3, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6. The cone of destructive energy the weapon creates increases to a 60-foot cone, the save DC increases to 18, and the damage increases to 12d6.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p. 25, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Dragon's Wrath Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. When it steeps in a dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the dragon's breath weapon and deals damage of that type with its special properties.
Slumbering (Uncommon): Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.
Stirring (Rare): The Stirring weapon has the Slumbering property. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra 1d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.
Wakened (Very Rare): The Wakened weapon has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring property. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +2, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6. As an action, you can unleash a 30-foot cone of destructive energy from the weapon. Each creature in that area must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Ascendant (Legendary): The Ascendant weapon has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring and Wakened properties. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +3, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6. The cone of destructive energy the weapon creates increases to a 60-foot cone, the save DC increases to 18, and the damage increases to 12d6.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p. 25, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Dragon's Wrath Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. When it steeps in a dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the dragon's breath weapon and deals damage of that type with its special properties.
Slumbering (Uncommon): Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.
Stirring (Rare): The Stirring weapon has the Slumbering property. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra 1d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.
Wakened (Very Rare): The Wakened weapon has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring property. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +2, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6. As an action, you can unleash a 30-foot cone of destructive energy from the weapon. Each creature in that area must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Ascendant (Legendary): The Ascendant weapon has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring and Wakened properties. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +3, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6. The cone of destructive energy the weapon creates increases to a 60-foot cone, the save DC increases to 18, and the damage increases to 12d6.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p. 25, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Slumbering Dragon's Wrath Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. When it steeps in a dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the dragon's breath weapon and deals damage of that type with its special properties. Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon.

item.name: Stirring Dragon's Wrath Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. When it steeps in a dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the dragon's breath weapon and deals damage of that type with its special properties. Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon. item.text:

item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. item.name: Ascendant Dragon's Wrath Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
This weapon is decorated with dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconic letters. When it steeps in a dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the dragon's breath weapon and deals damage of that type with its special properties. Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra 3d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon. As an action, you can unleash a 60-foot cone of destructive energy from the weapon. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 12d6 damage of the type dealt by the dragon's breath weapon on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p. 25, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Vicious Brass Knuckles +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. When you roll a 20 with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 slashing damage.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Acquisitions Incorporated p. 149, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Vicious Hand Claws +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. When you roll a 20 with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 slashing damage.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Acquisitions Incorporated p. 149, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Vicious Punch Knife +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. When you roll a 20 with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 slashing damage.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Acquisitions Incorporated p. 149, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Brass Knuckles +2 [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Hand Claws +2 [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Hand Claws +3 [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Armblade Punch Knife +3 [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
An armblade is a magic weapon that attaches to your arm, becoming inseparable from you as long as you're attuned to it. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your forearm for the entire attunement period. As a bonus action, you can retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Eberron: Rising from the Last War p. 276, Wayfinder's Guide to Eberron p. 120, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Ruidium Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This magic weapon has a dull, rusty color or has veins of ruidium running through it. While this weapon is on your person, you gain the following benefits:
• You can breathe water. • You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
Ruidium Strike: A creature you hit with this weapon takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage.
Ruidium Corruption: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll made with this weapon, you must make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. If you are not already suffering from ruidium corruption, you become corrupted when you fail this save.
If Ruidium Is Destroyed: If the Apotheon is killed or redeemed, all the ruidium in Exandria is destroyed instantly, and a ruidium weapon becomes a +2 weapon.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Critical Role: Call of the Netherdeep p. 216, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Ruidium Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
This magic weapon has a dull, rusty color or has veins of ruidium running through it. While this weapon is on your person, you gain the following benefits:
• You can breathe water. • You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
Ruidium Strike: A creature you hit with this weapon takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage.
Ruidium Corruption: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll made with this weapon, you must make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. If you are not already suffering from ruidium corruption, you become corrupted when you fail this save.
If Ruidium Is Destroyed: If the Apotheon is killed or redeemed, all the ruidium in Exandria is destroyed instantly, and a ruidium weapon becomes a +2 weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Critical Role: Call of the Netherdeep p. 216, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Ruidium Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
This magic weapon has a dull, rusty color or has veins of ruidium running through it. While this weapon is on your person, you gain the following benefits:
• You can breathe water. • You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
Ruidium Strike: A creature you hit with this weapon takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage.
Ruidium Corruption: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll made with this weapon, you must make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. If you are not already suffering from ruidium corruption, you become corrupted when you fail this save.
If Ruidium Is Destroyed: If the Apotheon is killed or redeemed, all the ruidium in Exandria is destroyed instantly, and a ruidium weapon becomes a +2 weapon.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Critical Role: Call of the Netherdeep p. 216, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Silvered Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Player's Handbook (2014) p. 148, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Silvered Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Player's Handbook (2014) p. 148, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Silvered Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Player's Handbook (2014) p. 148, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.

Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.

item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Punch Knife +3 [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Vicious Brass Knuckles [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
When you roll a 20 with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 slashing damage.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 209, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Vicious Hand Claws [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
When you roll a 20 with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 slashing damage.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 209, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Vicious Punch Knife [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
When you roll a 20 with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 slashing damage.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 209, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Brass Knuckles of Warning [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
This magic weapon warns you of danger. While the weapon is on your person, you have advantage on initiative rolls. In addition, you and any of your companions within 30 feet of you can't be surprised, except when incapacitated by something other than nonmagical sleep. The weapon magically awakens you and your companions within range if any of you are sleeping naturally when combat begins.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Hand Claws of Warning [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
This magic weapon warns you of danger. While the weapon is on your person, you have advantage on initiative rolls. In addition, you and any of your companions within 30 feet of you can't be surprised, except when incapacitated by something other than nonmagical sleep. The weapon magically awakens you and your companions within range if any of you are sleeping naturally when combat begins.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Punch Knife of Warning [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
This magic weapon warns you of danger. While the weapon is on your person, you have advantage on initiative rolls. In addition, you and any of your companions within 30 feet of you can't be surprised, except when incapacitated by something other than nonmagical sleep. The weapon magically awakens you and your companions within range if any of you are sleeping naturally when combat begins.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Dungeon Master's Guide (2014) p. 213, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Drow Brass Knuckles +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Push (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. This weapon loses its enchantment bonus permanently if it is exposed to sunlight for 1 hour or longer.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, brass knuckles Source: Monster Manual (2014) p. 126, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Drow Hand Claws +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. This weapon loses its enchantment bonus permanently if it is exposed to sunlight for 1 hour or longer.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, hand claws Source: Monster Manual (2014) p. 126, Ben Huffman - Pugilist

item.name: Drow Punch Knife +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. This weapon loses its enchantment bonus permanently if it is exposed to sunlight for 1 hour or longer.
Proficiency: simple, punch knife Source: Monster Manual (2014) p. 126, Ben Huffman - Pugilist
